latest.title: Annual Financial Report to 31 December 2025
latest.content:
Today 11:46
RNS Number : 4022K
R8 Capital Investments PLC
30 June 2026
30 June 2026
R8 CAPITAL INVESTMENTS PLC
("R8" or the "Company")
Annual Financial Report to 31 December 2025
The Company announces that its annual report and accounts for the period ending 31 December 2025 have been published on the Company's website
https://www.r8plc.com/investors/results-reports-presentations
and have been submitted and are available for inspection at the National Storage Mechanism
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
Further to the Company's announcement of 1 May 2026 regarding the temporary suspension of the listing and trading in the Company's ordinary shares, and following the publication of its audited Annual Report and Accounts for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, the Company will apply to the Financial Conduct Authority for the restoration of the listing of its ordinary shares and will seek the resumption of trading in its ordinary shares on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
Further announcements will follow shortly.
- ENDS -

latest.title: Suspension of Listing and Proposed Fundraising
latest.content:
Today 13:48
RNS Number : 8554C
R8 Capital Investments PLC
01 May 2026
1 May 2026
R8 Capital Investments Plc
(the "
Company
")
Suspension of Listing
and
Proposed Fundraising
FY25 Results and Suspension of Listing
Further to its announcement of 28 April 2026, the Company announces that as of 7:30am today, its securities of ordinary shares of £0.01 each have been temporarily suspended from listing in the Equity Shares (Shell Companies) Category of the Official List of the FCA and trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
The Company expects to publish its FY25 Results by the end of June and will make a further application to the FCA to lift the suspension on trading in its securities once the FY25 Results have been published.
The defined terms in the announcement of 28 April 2026 shall have the same meanings when used in this announcement.
Proposed Fundraising
The Company is pleased to announce it has entered into non-binding, conditional heads of terms for the proposed subscription by certain investors for a minimum of £500,000 in aggregate principal value for the issue of convertible loan notes by the Company terms to be agreed. The subscription is subject to, amongst other things, satisfactory KYC and AML being provided by the proposed investors. The Company anticipates completion of the subscription by 11 May 2026 and will accordingly update the market in due course.
Enquiries
Company
Jonathan Rowland / Richard Morecroft
info@r8plc.com

Corporate Broker
+ 44 (0) 20 7469 0930

latest.title: Audited Accounts to 31 December 2025 & Suspension
latest.content:
28 Apr 2026 12:56
RNS Number : 2543C
R8 Capital Investments PLC
28 April 2026
28 April 2026
R8 Capital Investments Plc
(the "
Company
")
Publication of the Annual Financial Report
for the year ended 31 December 2025
and
Temporary Suspension of Listing
R8 Capital Investments plc provides the following update regarding the publication of its annual financial report for the year ended 31 December 2025 ("
FY25 Results
").
The Company will not be in a position to publish its FY25 Results by close of business on 30 April 2026, being the latest date permitted for publication of the FY25 Results under DTR 4.1.3R.
The unforeseen delay to the audit is principally due to the ongoing wind-down of its Fibermode subsidiary which has extended the audit process.
The Company is working together with its auditors to finalise the audit and will provide a further update in due course. The revised date for publication of FY25 Results is a result of administrative delay only and does not indicate any irregularities in the FY25 Results.
The Company will therefore apply to the Financial Conduct Authority ("
FCA
") for the listing on the Official List of the FCA of its ordinary shares of £0.01 each to be temporarily suspended with effect from 7.30 am on 1 May 2026 until publication of the FY25 Results.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for the content of this announcement.

latest.content:

R8 Capital Investments PLC
25 March 2026
25 March 2026
R8 Capital Investments Plc
(the "
Company
")
Admission of Shares
Further to the Company's announcement on 27 June 2025, an application for Admission of the
2,619,782 ordinary
shares of £0.01 each in the share capital of the Company ("
Shares
")
to trading on the main market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange will be made and it is expected that admission of the Shares will become effective on or around 2 April 2026 and that dealings in the Shares will commence at that time.
As at the date of this announcement, the Company's issued ordinary share capital comprises 107,411,062 ordinary shares of £0.01 each. The above figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: MODE.L is a UK‑listed company that delivers software‑as‑a‑service solutions for businesses. Its product suite focuses on cloud‑based analytics and workflow automation, helping companies streamline operations and gain real‑time insights.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The firm operates in a high‑growth niche where digital transformation is accelerating. Its tools target mid‑market retailers and logistics firms, a segment that is moving quickly to adopt data‑driven decision making. The company’s technology can give its customers a competitive edge by reducing manual processes and cutting operating costs.
company_30s.current_state: MODE.L has struggled to turn a profit and is currently in a cash‑burn phase. It has recently been suspended from trading while it seeks additional capital and is working to stabilise its financials. The share price sits at a very low level compared to its historic highs.

rns_narrative: The company has been caught in a cycle of financial reporting and regulatory actions: a suspension of its listing in May, followed by a second suspension after new audited accounts were released in April. In March it announced interim results and a plan to raise funds, but the market has not yet reacted.

Across the last few months, the company has been trying to secure fresh capital, either through a private placement or a public offering, while also addressing the regulatory requirements that caused the suspensions.

There is no clear sign that the company has found a new revenue stream or that its product line has matured, but the repeated attempts at fundraising suggest that management believes the business model can eventually turn around.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are optimistic that the listing suspension will be lifted once the company raises the needed capital, and that a fresh influx of funds will allow it to finish product development and hit revenue targets.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most market participants worry that the company has a history of failed funding rounds, weak earnings, and repeated regulatory setbacks. They fear another suspension or outright delisting could wipe out the remaining equity.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat. There is very little new chatter, and the few posts that exist are largely cautious or mildly bullish. The community has not yet rallied around a clear thesis.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker are flat – no recent spikes in search activity, which means a news event could create a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: A successful funding round that lifts the listing suspension and provides working capital for product rollout.
An earnings announcement that shows a clear path to profitability or at least a reduction in the cash burn rate.
A strategic partnership or large client contract that validates the product offering and boosts revenue projections.
A regulatory decision to reinstate the stock on the exchange, which would open the market to new buyers.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden increase in trading volume, a break above the most recent low, and any announcement of capital injection or new client deals. A clear statement from the board about the status of the listing suspension is a critical signal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle. The APEX score indicates tight compression, suggesting the next move could be a breakout. Confirmation will come from volume spikes and a price move above the 20‑day moving average.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: MODE.L sits at a deep discount – roughly 90% below its recent peak – and has no obvious terminal risk. If the company secures funding and lifts the suspension, the price could rebound to 50% of its former high, delivering a 100% return. Even a modest 50% bounce would double an investment, while the downside is limited to the current price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest threat is that the company fails to raise capital or that regulators maintain the suspension, which would lead to a further decline or delisting. A continued cash burn could also push the price to zero if the business model never becomes viable.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Upside: 2‑5× the current price if the stock recovers to 50‑70% of its peak. Downside: the current price – a loss of the invested capital if the company collapses. The asymmetry is clear: the potential gain far outweighs the limited loss.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a breakout above the 20‑day moving average with volume at least 1.5× the 20‑day average. Size the position so that a 10% drop would trigger a stop, protecting the capital while giving the trade room to grow.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small position until a clear funding announcement or listing reinstatement occurs. Watch the volume and price action for a sustained move above the recent low; that will be the trigger for a full‑scale entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for insolvency, declares bankruptcy, or the regulator confirms permanent delisting, exit immediately and consider writing off the position.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MODE.L is a deeply discounted, distressed tech company that could rebound dramatically if it resolves its funding crisis; the upside is high, the downside is contained, making it a contrarian play worth watching.
